_extraction_note: Best-effort read of a dense chart. Bar-chart benchmark values are often visually ambiguous and have been omitted from strengths unless the bar label is clearly readable. Text in 'KEY SPECS' and 'WHY THIS MODEL' columns was preferred.

strengths: Jailbroken in Claude Code, multi-agent coordination
weaknesses: API only — no subscription plan for agents
claude-opus-4-7.use_for: Complex external dev via Claude Code, multi-file refactoring, vision-heavy agentic workflows, hours-long autonomous tasks

strengths: Absorbs GPT-5.3 Codex — dynamic MCP tool search, steerable computer use
Planner + judge in Codex, superhuman desktop control
gpt-5-4.use_for: External complex dev via Codex, vision agents, terminal-heavy workflows

weaknesses: Benchmarks self-reported by Z.AI (pending independent verification)
glm-5-1.use_for: Long-horizon agentic coding, sustained optimization loops, complex engineering over hours without human intervention

weaknesses: Proprietary — no open weights, API only
minimax-m2-7.use_for: OpenClaw execution backbone, Hermes high-volume agent tasks

strengths: Hybrid attention 7:1 ratio, MTP fast generation
mimo-v2-pro.use_for: Agent orchestration brain, custom OpenClaw workflows, terminal-heavy execution, long-context agent sessions

strengths: First open-weight model trained for parallel agentic work
weaknesses: ~6x more output tokens than peers — budget carefully
kimi-k2-5.use_for: Long-horizon task chains, research agents, multi-source browsing, parallel agentic work

strengths: DSA sparse attention, 3x faster long context
90% of GPT-5.4 performance at 1/50th the cost
deepseek-v3-2.use_for: High-volume coding pipelines, open-weight execution, cost-floor frontier reasoning

strengths: 98% of Opus coding at 1/5 the cost
Computer use 94% accuracy, OfficeQA matches Opus
1M context, prompt-injection resistance on par with Opus
claude-sonnet-4-6.use_for: Daily coding, content automation, Anthropic-ecosystem agents

gpt-5-4-mini.use_for: Hermes conscious layer, sub-agent delegation, 6-9 turn debates

strengths: Native multi-agent API (up to 16 parallel agents)
grok-4-20.use_for: Hallucination-sensitive pipelines, long-context research, parallel multi-agent workflows, X-data enriched agents

strengths: Native multimodal (text, image, video, audio) in one API call
No separate pipeline needed per modality
weaknesses: Dropped from Tier 1 — other options faster when multimodal not needed
gemini-3-1-pro.use_for: Multimodal agents, doc-heavy pipelines, video/audio processing, agents that need to see and hear

strengths: Hybrid linear attn + sparse MoE, build-in reasoning
Near-frontier agentic coding and front-end dev
qwen3-6-plus.use_for: Agentic coding, front-end dev, 1M context at low cost; free tier still available

strengths: EU/data sovereignty — self-host at zero marginal cost
llama-4-maverick.use_for: Self-hosted Role 3, EU/data residency needs, zero marginal cost at scale

strengths: Unifies Magistral + Pixtral + Devstral in one model
mistral-small-4.use_for: Budget-conscious pipelines, long-context research, replacing three specialist models with one endpoint

qwen3-5-9b.use_for: Always-on subconscious ideation loop, Hermes curation pass, $0 per call

qwen3-5-27b.use_for: Local summarization, routing decisions, complex micro classification

strengths: Dramatic leap over Gemma 3, best local reasoning benchmarks
gemma-4-31b.use_for: Local agentic sub-tasks, commercial deployment, math and reasoning needs

strengths: 32B dense distilled from R1 on Qwen3.5 base
OpenRouter free tier or ~$0.7/1M via API
weaknesses: Best for async tasks — latency tolerance needed
deepseek-r1-distill.use_for: Reasoning-heavy micro tasks, logic-based classification, math/logic, fallback

strengths: Purpose-built for agent tool use + web browsing
#1 OSS for agent tasks in its price tier
Designed from the ground up for agentic orchestration
weaknesses: Not a trimmed general model — narrow focus
glm-4-5-air.use_for: Lightweight agentic sub-tasks, web browsing agents, tool-call orchestration, cheap dispatcher
